chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يَنْقُضُ الْوُضُوءَ وَ مَا لَا يَنْقُضُهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 23 – What breaks the Ablution (Wudhu) and what does not break it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/115)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/115)
text: لَيْسَ يَنْقُضُ الْوُضُوءَ إِلَّا مَا خَرَجَ مِنْ طَرَفَيْكَ الْأَسْفَلَيْنِ اللَّذَيْنِ أَنْعَمَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْكَ بِهِمَا .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Al Fazl Bin Shazaan, and Ahmad Bin Idrees, from Muhammad Bin Abdul Jabbar, altogether from Safwan Bin Yahya, from Salim Abu Al Fazl,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘The Ablution (Wudhu) does not break except by what comes out from your two lower ends which Allahazwj has Favoured upon you by these two’.
en.sarwar: 1. Muhammad ibn ’Isma‘il has narrated from al-Fadl ibn Shadhan and Ahmad ibn Idris from Muhammad ibn ‘Abd al-Jabbar all from Safwan ibn Yahya from Salim abu al-Fadl who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Wudu’ does not become invalid unless something from your two passages of lower end, which Allah has granted you, comes out.’”
